stonetop.summary: If you descend from the Steplands to the southeast, you’ll find yourself on a rolling prairie.

text.content:
If you descend from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.No8UN1MJEb2mrLXo]{the Steplands} to the southeast, you’ll find yourself on a rolling prairie. The ground slopes gently down toward the east, to the banks of @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.lGNIQWIn8CPHuM7E]{the Dread River}.

Travelers and merchants aplenty cross these plains. They come north, from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.DPUvs6Ss8UHXEsCJ]{Lygos} and the civilized south, heading north to the Highway and from there to @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.uXlyry9CpXUz4ooR]{Marshedge}. Or they head the other way, bearing herbs and textiles, metals and gems, back to the southern cities. Or they come from beyond the Highway, from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.NERZ6p7FNc1pxNWL]{the North Manmarch}, to hunt or trade or raid.

But for all those who pass through the South Manmarch, few call it home. Sure, the Hillfolk graze their herds here in the warmer months. And some new settlement is always popping up. But the land is dotted with burnt-out homesteads and ruined forts: a mute testament to those who have tried to settle here before. Tried, and failed.

Always

  • The eternal, unresponsive sky
  • Shallow, rocky soil broken by outcrops of white limestone and gray shale
  • Smoke from a lone homestead, overlooking a watercourse
  • A stream or dry gully, winding lazily down toward @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.lGNIQWIn8CPHuM7E]{the Dread River}
  • An abandoned, collapsed longhouse
  • A Hillfolk horseband, watching from higher ground
  • Spring
  • A mottled carpet of new grass and wildflowers—yellow, pale green, lilac
  • Pocket wetlands, home to nesting ducks recently returned from the south
  • A great cloud of birds, bursting forth from the brush
  • Summer
  • Squinting into a blinding sky
  • The fervid and unrelenting chorus of summer insects, shifting in tone and rhythm with time of day
  • Tallgrass in full flush, waist-high and rippling in a southerly wind.
  • A mob of white-tailed deer or mighty elk, grazing on a long slope
  • Autumn
  • Grassheads gone rust-colored, stalks still green
  • A streamside stand of cottonwoods, glowing gold in the dusk light
  • Winter
  • Days of scouring, southwesterly wind
  • Powdery snowdrifts, stretching leeward from ripples in the landscape
  • Patches of dead grass, showing orange through the snow

Before the Cataclysm @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.lGNIQWIn8CPHuM7E]{the Dread River} formed the eastern border of Stone Lord territory. After the Stone Lords fell, the Barrow Builders fought each other back and forth across this expanse, grinding themselves down, like shards of shale abraded to dust over time.

Now, the South Manmarch is the border between the civilized south and the hinterlands of the World’s End. It’s a lawless place, full of dangerous beasts and even more dangerous people. Nomadic Hillfolk claim much of the region, and bandits are a constant threat.

And yet, the lure of the prairie is great. Every year or so, someone tries to carve out a piece of for themselves. A new settlement might be:

  • A family from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.uXlyry9CpXUz4ooR]{Marshedge} or @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.NERZ6p7FNc1pxNWL]{the North Manmarch}, starting their own farm
  • An ambitious Lygosi merchant, investing in a new trading post or caravansary
  • Some would-be chief or warlord from the Manmarchers, building a new hamlet or hillfort
  • The conquering armies of the South, establishing a foothold

Such settlements rarely last. They fall prey to natural disaster or roving beasts, to rivals or bandits, or to the certain wrath of the Hillfolk who claim these lands as theirs.

Or perhaps, if you believe the stories, there’s something darker afoot. Perhaps these lands really are haunted. Haunted by the numerous souls who’ve died here. Or by foul things, slithering out of the Dread River. Perhaps the land is cursed, and no settlement can ever survive. Perhaps.

Draining out of @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.No8UN1MJEb2mrLXo]{the Steplands} and into @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.lGNIQWIn8CPHuM7E]{the Dread River}, this watercourse draws thickets, copses of cottonwood, wildlife, and wanderers to its gentle banks. The Hillfolk call it Maengwalc in their own tongue—“Flintwash,” after the stony plateau from which its headwaters spring.

Hundreds of rills and brooks feed the Flintwash, and in its lower reaches, it’s quite broad and difficult to cross. Yet ways do exist. How might a traveler get from one side to the other? Ask a PC who would know, but perhaps there is…

  • … an old bridge from the time of the Makers; … a newer bridge, built at great expense by some southern merchant or noble; … a shallow ford; wide, rocky and usually safe; … a ferry, run by enterprising souls; … some combination of the above; or … something else entirely.

• Who did you know who tried to settle in the South Manmarch? What fate befell them?
  • What bandit crew is currently the most feared? Who leads them, and what makes them so dangerous?
  • At what landmark do merchant caravans typically encamp? Why do they stop there?
  • What landmark does everyone who travels these lands know to avoid? Why?

• Someone has seized the Flintwash crossing, and is charging exorbitant tolls
  • An ambitious chief from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.NERZ6p7FNc1pxNWL]{the North Manmarch} is sending settlers south of the Highway
  • The PCs eagerly await a caravan, but it gets waylaid by bandits
  • A ruin in the region becomes a place of pilgrimage, drawing the faithful of Helior/Aratis from all over

Pick 1 or combine 2, or have someone roll the Die of Fate (d12) once or twice.

1d12 terrain

RollResult
1Ash field/barrens/sand/rocky stretch
2Burrow/dugout/warren
3Sodden soil/mud/pocket wetland
4Spring/pond/creek/stream/river
5–6Open meadow, waist-high grass
7–8Shortgrass prairie, vast and open
9Footpath/wagon ruts/trail
10Tree(s), savanna, thickets
11Gentle slope/low ridge/small hill
12Steep slope/rocky outcrop/large hill

If you like, roll 1d6 for size/scope/intensity.

For any one of these, pick 1 or have someone roll the Die of Fate.

1d6 discovery

RollResult
1Change of terrain and roll again
2A chance for insight into a threat or danger (e.g. tracks that reveal numbers, whereabouts, etc.)
3Useful or valuable flora
4beast (see below)
5An encounter (see below)
6A point of interest

1d6 beast

RollResult
1–3Mostly harmless but prone to bolt: deer, hares, prairie dogs, birds, etc.
4–5A herd: aurochs, goats, sheep, wild horses, etc.
6Something big and majestic: a brawzbedr, a giant ibis, a horned drake, a thunder drake, etc.

1d6 encounter

RollResult
1Hillfolk: encamped, herding, hunting, on patrol, come to trade, or maybe on a raid
2Merchant caravan, coming or going
3Bandits, encamped, scouting, returning from a raid
4Settlers, hoping to carve out a home here
5Someone in dire straits; roll 1d4 again to see who
6Spirit of the wild, curious or indifferent

1d6 point of interest

RollResult
1A place corrupted by the Things Below
2A tomb, site, or artifact of the Barrow Builders
3A battlefield or remains (below)
4A ruin or fortification of the Stone Lords
5A place sacred to the spirits of the wild
6A new settlement

1d12 battlefield or remains

RollResult
1Truly ancient: from the time of the Makers
2From the Time of Cataclysm, when humanity rebelled and woke the Things Below
3–4From a conflict among the Barrow Builders
5–7From a previous attempt at settlement: burnt out, collapsed, overgrown
8–10Recent: Hillfolk, bandits, travelers, settlers, beasts
11–12Very recent: the buzzards and crows still feasting

Many such places are haunted by the dead.

Hazards

  • Baking sun, no shade for miles
  • Nasty turn in the weather: lashing wind, bitter cold, blistering heat, sleet/ hail/rain/snow; lightning
  • Flash flooding in valleys/on slopes
  • Lack of shelter, water, or wood for a long-burning fire (try dried aurochs dung, if you can find it)
  • Hidden holes/burrows, twisting an ankle or worse
  • Prairie fire

Creatures the bestiary catalogues in and around South Manmarch. Click through for full stat blocks and lore.

  • @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-bestiary-journal.JournalEntry.VJDP2ETcmA8GeHdT]{Horned Drake}
